# Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) / SPA Pre-Stage (Secondary Market Sale)
**Purpose:** To record the agreed commercial terms and binding obligations between Seller and Buyer for a property sale prior to transfer/registration, including deposits, timelines, defaults, and broker commission.

## 1. Parties
Date: ____ / ____ / ______  
MOU Reference (CRM/ERP): __________________________

Seller: __________________________  
Emirates ID / Passport / Trade License No.: __________________________  
Address: ___________________________________________  
Mobile/Email: ___________________________________________

Buyer: __________________________  
Emirates ID / Passport / Trade License No.: __________________________  
Address: ___________________________________________  
Mobile/Email: ___________________________________________

Broker: Mi Casa Real Estate, CN-3762725, Abu Dhabi, UAE (the “Broker”).

## 2. Property
Property Address / Location: ___________________________________________  
Building / Community: ___________________________________________  
Unit/Villa No.: ___________________________  
Property Type: ___________________________  
Title Deed / Reference (if available): ___________________________  
Current Status: ☐ Vacant ☐ Tenanted (Expiry: ____ / ____ / ______) ☐ Owner Occupied  
Included Items (if any): ___________________________________________

## 3. Purchase Price and Payment
3.1 Purchase Price: AED ___________________________ (the “Price”).  
3.2 Deposit: AED ___________________________ payable on signing this MOU.  
Deposit Paid To: ☐ Seller ☐ Broker (only if separately authorized in writing) ☐ Other: __________  
3.3 Balance: AED ___________________________ payable on transfer/registration completion (or as otherwise agreed): ___________________________________________.  
3.4 Costs: Unless otherwise agreed in writing, each party bears its own advisor costs. Transfer/registration fees and related administrative charges shall be borne by: ☐ Buyer ☐ Seller ☐ Shared (details): ___________________________.

## 4. Conditions and Seller Obligations
4.1 The Seller shall provide the Buyer (or Buyer’s bank) with copies of required documents promptly, including: title deed/reference, Seller ID, and any other documents reasonably required for transfer.  
4.2 The Seller shall obtain and provide any required NOC/clearances from relevant entities (developer/management/authority) as needed for transfer, within a reasonable timeframe and in any event no later than: ____ / ____ / ______ (unless delays occur beyond Seller’s control and are promptly evidenced).  
4.3 The Seller confirms that the Seller has authority to sell the Property and that the Property is not subject to undisclosed restrictions preventing sale, except as disclosed here: ___________________________________________.

## 5. Buyer Obligations
5.1 The Buyer shall provide required identification, KYC, and source-of-funds evidence where reasonably requested and shall cooperate with bank/mortgage processes (if applicable).  
5.2 If the Buyer is using mortgage finance, the Buyer shall use best efforts to obtain final approval by: ____ / ____ / ______. The Buyer must keep the Seller informed of progress.

## 6. Transfer/Completion Timeline
6.1 The parties agree to complete transfer/registration by: ____ / ____ / ______ (the “Completion Date”), subject to required approvals and administrative availability.  
6.2 The parties shall attend and sign required documents and shall not unreasonably delay completion.

## 7. Possession and Handover
7.1 Vacant possession shall be provided on completion unless otherwise agreed: ☐ Yes ☐ No (details): ___________________________.  
7.2 Handover items (keys/access cards/parking remotes/manuals) shall be delivered on completion.

## 8. Broker Commission and VAT
8.1 The Seller and/or Buyer agree that brokerage commission is payable as follows:  
Payable By: ☐ Seller ☐ Buyer ☐ Both (split): ___________________________  
Commission Rate/Amount: ___________________________  
8.2 If VAT is applicable, the commission is: ☐ exclusive of VAT ☐ inclusive of VAT.  
8.3 The parties acknowledge that the Broker’s commission is earned upon signing of this MOU (or other binding sale agreement) by the parties for this Property, and remains payable even if the parties later attempt to complete outside the Broker after introduction and negotiation through the Broker.

## 9. Default and Remedies
9.1 Buyer Default: If the Buyer fails to complete due to Buyer’s breach (excluding proven Seller breach), then:  
(a) the Seller may terminate this MOU by written notice; and  
(b) the deposit shall be: ☐ forfeited to Seller ☐ partially forfeited (details): ___________________________ ☐ refunded (details): ___________________________.  
9.2 Seller Default: If the Seller fails to complete due to Seller’s breach (excluding proven Buyer breach), then:  
(a) the Buyer may terminate this MOU by written notice; and  
(b) the deposit shall be refunded to the Buyer, and the Seller shall pay documented direct losses up to AED __________ (if agreed) or as determined by competent authority/court.  
9.3 Mutual Termination / Force Majeure / Administrative Delay: If completion becomes impossible due to reasons beyond both parties’ control (documented), the parties shall cooperate in good faith to reschedule. If the parties agree to terminate, the deposit shall be returned to the Buyer unless otherwise agreed in writing.

## 10. Representations and No Reliance
10.1 The Seller represents that the Seller will not knowingly provide misleading information.  
10.2 The Buyer acknowledges that the Buyer has had the opportunity to inspect the Property and obtain independent advice, and is not relying on any representation not expressly stated in this MOU.

## 11. Confidentiality and Data Handling
The parties shall treat personal data and transaction documents as confidential and shall use them only for completing the transaction, subject to applicable UAE data protection requirements.

## 12. Notices
Notices must be in writing and delivered by email and/or hand delivery to the contact details stated above (or updated in writing).

## 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
This MOU is governed by the laws of the United Arab Emirates and the Emirate of Abu Dhabi. The courts of Abu Dhabi shall have jurisdiction.

## 14. Entire Agreement
This MOU constitutes the entire agreement between Buyer and Seller regarding the sale of the Property at this stage and supersedes prior communications, except for documents expressly referenced herein.
